GEOTECHNICAL ENGINEERING CONSTRUCTION OBSERVATION 
ANCHOR INSTALLATION AND TESTING 
KUHIO HIGHWAY EMERGENCY SLOPE STABILIZATION 
IN THE VICINITY OF KALIHIWAI BRIDGE 
FEDERAL-AID PROJECT NO. ER-16(003) 
HANALEI, KAUAI, HAWAII 
Dear Mr. Miyahara: 
This letter report summarizes our geotechnical engineering observation services 
performed during the installation and testing of the pre-production sacrificial and 
production anchors for the Kuhio Highway Emergency Slope Stabilization in the Vicinity 
of Kalihiwai Bridge project located in the District of Hanalei on the Island of Kauai, Hawaii.  
The project location and general vicinity are shown on the Project Location Map, Plate 1. 
In general, this letter report presents our observations and the results of the 
grouted anchor installation and testing performed for the project from August 3, 2020 
through April 23, 2021. HI-TECH Rockfall Construction, Inc. was the general contractor 
responsible for installation and testing of the anchors. 
PROJECT CONSIDERATIONS 
The Kalihiwai Slope Stabilization project is located along the south side of Kuhio 
Highway east of the Kalihiwai Bridge on the Island of Kauai, Hawaii. Due to heavy rains 
during the months of February and March 2012, slope failures occurred along the 
adjacent roadway slopes. This project involves slope scaling and the installation of 
anchored wire mesh and draped wire mesh for remedial measures of the failed slope 
areas. 
Geolabs, Inc. conducted a geotechnical engineering exploration of the project site, 
and our recommendations are presented in the report entitled “Geotechnical Engineering 
Exploration, Kuhio Highway (Route 56), Emergency Slope Stabilization in the Vicinity of 
Kalihiwai Bridge, Federal Aid Project No. ER-16(003), Island of Kauai, Hawaii,” dated 
October 6, 2017. The purpose of our geotechnical engineering services during the 
construction of the project was to observe the construction and testing of the soil/rock 
anchors for general conformance with the project plans and specifications. 
KSF, Inc. 
Page 2 
W.O. 6731-10 
December 3, 2021 
GEOLABS, INC. 
Hawaii • California 
SLOPE SCALING 
A Geolabs representative observed the debris and loose rock removal operations 
as part of the project requirements during the slope scaling phase of this project. Slope 
scaling operations were conducted by HI-TECH Rockfall Construction, Inc. and 
subcontractor JP’s Tree Trimming LLC. The general contractor utilized repelling with 
ropes to access areas of the slope and hand tools to remove unstable rock or soil. The 
subcontractor utilized an Altec boom truck equipped with gas-powered hand tools and 
rappelling equipment to access the slope from above and remove trees or branches 
considered to be of risk. Debris at the toe of the slope was removed by the general 
contractor utilizing a Komatsu PC138 and a CAT 308 E2 excavator. Slope scaling 
operations were monitored from April 10, 2020 to July 24, 2020. 
ANCHORED WIRE MESH ANCHOR INSTALLATION AND TESTING 
As part of the project requirements, an anchored wire mesh system was designed, 
consisting of 361 individual reinforcing bars grouted into drilled holes to stabilize the 
existing slope. Boundary ropes and supplemental anchors were used to pull the edge of 
the anchored wire mesh tight against the slope for aesthetic appearance. 
Due to existing slope conditions after tree clearing and slope scaling, the Anchor Wire 
Mesh system was extended up approximately 30 feet in elevation to the top of the existing 
slope from the previously estimated elevation of +170 feet Mean Sea Level (MSL) to 
approximately +200 feet MSL and to the west of the existing edge of the slope facing 
Kuhio Highway. 
The grouted soil/rock installations, verification, and proof tests were performed by 
HI-TECH Rockfall Construction, Inc. The approximate locations of the grouted rock anchors 
are shown on the Anchor Location Plan, Plates 2.1 through 2.3. Installation and testing of 
the anchors were conducted from August 18, 2020 to February 23, 2021. Summaries of the 
anchor installations and testing for the anchored wire mesh are presented on Plates 3.1 
through 3.12. The installation records are provided on Plates 4.1 through 4.720. 
Grouted Anchor Installation 
A Geolabs representative observed the drilling and grouting operations during the 
construction of the soil/rock anchors. The anchors were drilled using a winch drill rig and an 
excavator-mounted drill rig, each equipped with a down-the-hole hammer and a 4-inch 
button hammer bit. 
The anchor holes were drilled to a depth of approximately 13 feet from the slope face 
with an approximate inclination of about 20 degrees from horizontal. The anchor holes at 
the top of the slope and at the intermediate bench at approximate Elevation +130 feet MSL 
were drilled to a depth of about 13 feet from the slope face with an approximate inclination 
of about 75 degrees from horizontal.  
KSF, Inc. 
Page 3 
W.O. 6731-10 
December 3, 2021 
GEOLABS, INC. 
Hawaii • California 
After the hole was drilled to the specified depth, the Grade 75, #11 threaded bar was 
inserted into the drilled hole.  Centralizers were placed along the rebar at 5-foot intervals. 
Grout began progressively from bottom to top through the grouting tube until fresh grout 
appeared at the top of the hole. After completion of the grouting operation, the grouting tube 
remained grouted in the hole along with the anchor tendon. The grout was designed with a 
minimum 28-day compressive strength of 4,000 psi. 
Grouted Anchor Testing 
In accordance with the project specifications, the pre-production sacrificial anchor 
shall be loaded to 200 percent of the design load (15.0 kips). Based on the project 
specifications, 10 percent of the production anchors (36 anchors) were selected for proof 
testing. Proof test anchors were loaded to 133 percent of the design load (22.5 kips). 
The pre-production anchor was tested on August 17, 2020.  The testing apparatus 
included a 60-ton hydraulic jack (Enerpac RCH603 – S/N 749511) and a 1-inch stroke 
dial gauge. The test was conducted by pre-loading the testing apparatus up to about 
10 kips to engage the reaction timbers and ground. The pre-production anchor was then 
loaded up to about 200 percent of the design load. The anchor was tested to 31 kips with 
a total displacement of about 1.038 inches.  Details of the pre-production anchor testing 
are presented on Plates 5.1 through 5.4. 
In accordance with the project specifications, 10 percent of the total production 
anchors were proof tested from November 11 to February 26, 2021. The following table 
summarizes the anchors tested on each test date. The maximum total head movement 
was about 0.569 inches from Anchor 1F. Results of the proof tests, including Load versus 
Displacement and Creep Movement versus Time curves, are presented on Plates 6.1 
through 6.144.

DRAPED WIRE MESH ANCHOR INSTALLATION AND TESTING 
As part of the project requirements, a draped wire mesh system was installed 
consisting of 47 individual grouted anchors with a depth of 20 feet at the top of the slope. 
Bottom wire anchors with boundary ropes were installed at lengths of about 5 to 7 feet 
and grouted into the drilled holes and were used to pull the edge of the draped wire mesh 
tight against the slope for aesthetic appearance. Both top and bottom anchors were 
installed at about 20 feet spacing across the at-risk areas. In addition, supplemental 
(short) anchors with additional boundary rope were installed where a smooth slope 
surface could not be achieved and used to push the wire mesh against the ground to 
develop this smooth surface. A summary of the draped wire mesh installation and testing 
are presented on Plates 7.1 through 7.3.    
Grouted Anchor Installation 
A Geolabs representative observed the drilling and grouting operations during the 
construction of the soil/rock anchors. The anchors were drilled using a winch drill rig and 
were equipped with a down-the-hole hammer and a 4-inch underreamer percussion bit for 
the top anchors. 
The anchors were drilled to a depth of approximately 21 feet from the front faces of 
the slope facing with an inclination of about 75 degrees from horizontal. After the holes were 
drilled to the specified depth and cleaned out, a Grade 75, #11 threaded bar with a 0.5-inch 
diameter high-density plastic tremie pipe was inserted into the drilled hole. Centralizers were 
placed along the threaded bar at 5-foot intervals. 
Grouting began progressively from bottom to top through the grout tube until fresh 
grout appeared at the top of the hole. After completion of the grouting operation, the grout 
KSF, Inc. 
Page 5 
W.O. 6731-10 
December 3, 2021 
GEOLABS, INC. 
Hawaii • California 
tube remained grouted in the hole along with the anchor tendon. The grout was designed 
with a minimum 28-day compressive strength of 4,000 psi. Anchored wire mesh installation 
logs are presented on Plates 8.1 through 8.182. 
Grouted Anchor Testing 
Based on the project specifications, 20 percent of the total number of grouted rock 
anchors were selected for testing. Anchors that were selected for proof testing are TD-2, 
TD-5, TD-7, TD-17, TD-21, TD-27, TD-28, TD-32, TD-36, and TD-40.  Results from the 
grouted anchor testing are presented on Plates 9.1 through 9.40. Proof test anchors were 
loaded to 133 percent of the design load of 20 kips. At this point, the load was maintained 
for 60 minutes to measure the creep movements. The criteria of the grouted anchors 
consist of the following: 
For the proof tests, a total creep movement of less than 0.04 inch is 
measured between the 1- and 10-minute readings or a total creep 
movement of less than 0.04 inch is measured between the 6- and 60-minute 
readings and the creep rate is linear or decreasing throughout the creep 
test load hold period. 
Total measured movement at the maximum test load exceeds 80 percent 
of the theoretical elastic elongation of the test nail unbonded length. 
Pullout failure does not occur at the maximum test load. 
Results of the proof tests, including Load versus Displacement and Creep 
Movement versus Time curves, are presented on Plates 9.1 through 9.40. 
Based on our observations, we believe that the anchors for both the draped wire 
mesh and the anchor wired mesh systems observed by Geolabs have been installed in 
general conformance with the project plans and specifications and within the limits of 
generally accepted construction practices. Based on our observations of the grouted 
anchor tests performed by the contractor, it is our professional opinion that the anchors 
have been installed to provide sufficient load capacity with tolerable movements.  
LIMITATIONS 
It should be noted that it is not possible to continuously observe all activities 
occurring simultaneously on a construction site; therefore, some of the work for the 
grouted anchor installation and testing may have occurred without our direct observation.  
However, we believe that the level of geotechnical observation provided is reasonable in 
reducing the geotechnical risks to the project. 
Our services were performed in accordance with generally accepted standards of 
geotechnical engineering practices; no warranty is expressed or implied.  Provision of 
construction monitoring by an engineer is not insurance, nor does it guarantee construction 
KSF, Inc. Page 6
W.O. 6731-10
December 3, 2021
of any type. The contractor shall retain responsibility for the quality of their work, for adhering
to the plans and specifications, and for repairing defects regardless of when they are found.
CLOSURE
We appreciate the opportunity to provide geotechnical engineering services to
KSF, Inc. on this project. If you have questions or need additional information, please
contact our office.

1A
Engineer:
Method:
Rig Type:
Air Pressure (psi):
4
1
Drilling was done using a Trolly/Winch Rig
2
Air pressure was supplied by a AtlasCopco Tier 4 compressor.
3
There was no temporary steel casing beside the rods in the drill hole.
4
Drilling began on 8/27/20 at 11:52 and ended at 12:13.
5
The drilling was easy difficulty and the cuttings were mostly brown.

Grout was placed thru a 1/2" diameter high density plastic tremie pipe.

4
1
Drilling was done using a Trolly/Winch Rig
2
Air pressure was supplied by a (1) AtlasCopco Tier4 & (2) AIRMAN PDS400S compressors. 
3
There was no temporary steel casing beside the rods in the drill hole.
4
Drilling began on 10/22/20 at 11:35 and ended at 12:14.
5
The drilling was in between medium hard and hard; the cuttings were mostly brownish gray.

10Q
Engineer:
Method:
Rig Type:
Air Pressure (psi):
4
1
Drilling was done using a Trolly/Winch Rig
2
Air pressure was supplied by a AtlasCopco Tier 4 compressor.
3
There was no temporary steel casing beside the rods in the drill hole.
4
Drilling began on 9/9/20 at 13:45 and ended at 14:11.
5
The drilling was hard difficulty and the cuttings were mostly gray.

There was no temporary steel casing beside the rods in the drill hole.
4

5
The drilling was medium-hard difficulty and the cuttings were mostly brownish gray.

Air pressure was supplied by a (1) AtlasCopco Tier4 & (2) AIRMAN PDS400S compressors. 
3
There was no temporary steel casing beside the rods in the drill hole.
4
Drilling began on 2/18/21 at 14:15 and ended at 14:26.
5
The drilling was medium-hard difficulty and the cuttings were mostly brown.

minutes, contractor decides to temporarily stop

continuing grout placement in TD-2.
14:17
14:25
Grouting
Note:
A potential void was encountered which took up about
30 bags to fill anchor hole and void space. Anchor was
selected for proof test to determine if it meets required
specs for the Draped Wire Mesh System.
